name: Dext Expense Capture and Bookkeeping API
what_it_does: Dext (formerly Receipt Bank) expense capture and bookkeeping automation REST API for accounting firms, bookkeepers, and SMBs to automate receipt scanning, expense extraction, and accounting software synchronization. Enables AI agents to manage receipt and invoice image submission for OCR extraction automation, handle extracted line item data retrieval (supplier, amount, tax, date) for expense categorization automation, access client document inbox management for accounting firm client data automation, retrieve bank statement import and transaction matching for bookkeeping automation, manage supplier rules and category assignment for automated expense coding automation, handle expense report approval workflow for corporate expense management automation, access VAT/GST tax code extraction for tax compliance automation, retrieve integration with QuickBooks, Xero, and Sage for direct accounting software posting automation, manage client portal and team access for accounting practice management automation, and integrate Dext with accounting software and corporate card platforms for end-to-end expense-to-ledger automation.
use_cases: Scanning receipts from expense capture automation agents
Extracting invoice data from bookkeeping automation agents
Syncing to accounting software from AP workflow agents
Managing client documents from accounting firm practice agents
not_for: Enterprise ERP procurement and PO management (use SAP Ariba or Coupa)
Corporate travel booking (use Concur or TripActions)
Full accounts payable with payment execution (use Bill.com or Tipalti)
best_when: An accounting firm, bookkeeper, or SMB wanting AI agents to automate receipt capture, OCR extraction, expense categorization, and accounting software synchronization using Dext's bookkeeping automation platform.
avoid_when: OCR EXTRACTION ACCURACY FOR AUTOMATED POSTING: Dext OCR extraction accuracy varies by document quality, language, and supplier format; automated accounting software posting without human review of extracted data creates incorrect ledger entries for poor-quality receipts; implement confidence score threshold review before automated posting for unreviewed extraction results. VAT/GST PARTIAL EXEMPTION FOR MULTI-JURISDICTION BUSINESSES: Automated VAT code extraction by Dext may not correctly handle partial exemption or mixed supply businesses; automated VAT posting without accountant review of tax code assignment creates VAT return errors; partial exemption businesses require manual tax code review even with automated extraction. GDPR DATA PROCESSOR ROLE FOR CLIENT DOCUMENT STORAGE: Dext stores client receipt and invoice images as data processor; accounting firms using Dext API for client data processing must maintain data processing agreements with clients; automated client document ingestion without GDPR data processing agreement creates controller-processor compliance gap for EU clients.

auth.notes: Dext uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. REST API with JSON. London, UK HQ. Founded 2010 as Receipt Bank by Michael Wood and Alexis Prenn. Rebranded to Dext in 2021 after acquiring Xavier Analytics and Greenback. Backed by Insight Partners ($165M raised). 700,000+ SMBs served through 14,000+ accounting partner firms. Integration: QuickBooks Online, Xero, Sage, FreshBooks, MYOB. GDPR. SOC2. Mobile receipt capture apps for iOS/Android. Competes with Hubdoc (Xero-owned) and AutoEntry (Sage-owned) for bookkeeping automation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-user monthly subscription with document volume tiers
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-user per-month subscription; Starter (individual), Teams (5+ users), Partner (accounting firm multi-client); document volume add-ons
pricing.notes: London UK. Insight Partners backed. Per-user subscription. Accounting firm partner channel. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; document submission and retrieval throttled; webhooks for document processing completion and accounting software sync events

known_agent_gotchas: OCR PROCESSING ASYNC WEBHOOK PATTERN: Dext document OCR extraction is asynchronous; document submission returns a document ID but extracted data is not immediately available; automated data pipelines must listen for webhook events for extraction completion before fetching results; synchronous document-to-data pipelines that poll immediately after submission will receive incomplete extraction

DUPLICATE DOCUMENT DETECTION TOLERANCE: Dext duplicate detection uses image hash comparison; automated receipt ingestion from multiple sources (email forwarding, mobile app, API) can create duplicate documents if the same receipt is submitted via multiple channels; implement document hash deduplication in automated ingestion layer before Dext submission to avoid duplicate accounting entries

SUPPLIER RULE TRAINING DATA DEPENDENCY: Dext automated category assignment relies on supplier rules configured per client; automated expense categorization for new suppliers without existing rules requires fallback category; automated posting of uncategorized items creates accounting software entries in default catch-all category requiring manual reclassification

ACCOUNTING SOFTWARE POSTING REVERSAL COMPLEXITY: Dext posts to accounting software (Xero, QuickBooks) as published transactions; automated posting errors require manual reversal in the accounting software — Dext API does not support automated reversal of already-posted transactions; implement pre-posting validation before automated accounting software publication

MULTI-CURRENCY FOREIGN EXCHANGE RATE SOURCE: Dext applies exchange rates for multi-currency receipts from its own rate feed; automated multi-currency expense reporting must reconcile Dext's applied rate with the corporate card FX rate to avoid P&L currency variance; automated multi-currency posting without rate source reconciliation creates accounting restatement risk for companies with material foreign currency expenses
agent_readiness.security_notes: Expense capture and bookkeeping. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2/API key. EU/UK/AU. Financial documents, receipt images, and accounting data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Bookkeeping automation. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2/API key. EU/UK/AU. Financial documents and accounting data.
